mnemonics: Matrix '1's Point Paths (M1PP): A '1' in a zero-one matrix at (i, j) points a path from node i to node j on its directed graph.
keyPoints: A relation can be represented equivalently by a zero-one matrix (a table of 1s and 0s) or a directed graph (a set of points with arrows connecting them).
A '1' in the matrix at position (row i, column j) means there is a directed edge from node i to node j in the graph.
Matrix operations like join, meet, and Boolean product correspond to set operations (union, intersection) and composition of paths on the graph.
commonMistakes: Confusing the row and column indices when translating between the matrix and graph representations (e.g., thinking a '1' at (i,j) means an edge from j to i).
Forgetting that the Boolean product used for composition is based on logical OR and AND, not standard matrix multiplication.
